description: A bird crossing a valley without touching the ground is 'wapi'. The verb follows movement through open air without surface support, whether the subject is a bird, seed, or kite. 'wapho' (throw) names a launching act; 'wapi' follows the resulting airborne motion.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then open the mouth into stressed central 'a'. Close the lips for unvoiced 'p', release them cleanly, and spread them slightly for high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: The rounded opening releases into broad 'a', then the lips close and let the compact final syllable spring into bright 'i'. That small lift is also heard at the start of 'wapho' (throw), an audible family resemblance within Phi.
usage_notes: The subject may supply its own lift or be carried by wind. Direction and destination use the ordinary motion prepositions, while height remains a separate spatial description.

semantic_domains.activity: movement through air by a living or carried subject
semantic_domains.nature: the ordinary flight of birds and wind-borne living material
semantic_domains.physical: a body held aloft without surface support
semantic_domains.spatial: an airborne path across a place
